---
title: "S3_to_Spark"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#Initialize Spark Session----
if (exists("sc")) {
  spark_disconnect(sc)
}

conf <- spark_config()
conf$spark.app.name <- "NYC Taxi"
conf$spark.hadoop.fs.s3a.endpoint <- "s3.amazonaws.com"
conf$spark.hadoop.fs.s3a.impl <- "org.apache.hadoop.fs.s3a.S3AFileSystem"
conf$spark.hadoop.fs.s3a.aws.credentials.provider <- "org.apache.hadoop.fs.s3a.AnonymousAWSCredentialsProvider"
conf$spark.sql.parquet.enableVectorizedReader <- "false"

sc <- spark_connect(master = "local", version = "3.5.0", config = conf)

yellow_read_schema <- list(
  # Original Columns (now forced to double)
  VendorID              = "double",
  tpep_pickup_datetime  = "timestamp",
  tpep_dropoff_datetime = "timestamp",
  passenger_count       = "double",
  trip_distance         = "double",
  PULocationID          = "double",
  DOLocationID          = "double",
  payment_type          = "double",
  fare_amount           = "double",
  tip_amount            = "double",
  total_amount          = "double",
  
  # Engineered Columns (created in your loop)
  year                  = "double",
  month                 = "double",
  total_fees            = "double"
)
```



```{r}
#main directory (all files)
#s3_file_directory <- "s3a://cs4265-nyc-taxi-project/data/yellow_split/"

#directory for much smaller set of test files (proof of concept)
s3_file_directory <- "s3a://cs4265-nyc-taxi-project/data/yellow_test/" 

yellow_s3_data <- spark_read_parquet(
  sc = sc,
  name = "yellow_s3_data",
  path = s3_file_directory,
  columns = yellow_read_schema,
  options = list(mergeSchema = "false"),
  memory = FALSE
)

#yellow_normalized <- yellow_s3_data |>
#  mutate(
#    # Ensure IDs are doubles (helpful for joins later)
#    VendorID     = as.double(VendorID),
#    PULocationID = as.double(PULocationID),
#    DOLocationID = as.double(DOLocationID)
#  )

print(yellow_s3_data)
```
